justifications.clarity: The NL statement is clear once SI and φ are known, but assumes reader knows Structural Importance.
justifications.novelty: Genuinely new — distinguishes f (true structure) from f̂ (predictor), an asymmetry missing from prior axioms.
justifications.internal_consistency: Quantifiers are tight; no scope error; the implication is well-formed.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Has explicit Heskes 2021 citation with page/line; anchors are well populated; some supporters are implicit rather than direct.
justifications.actionability: Directly testable: build f with SI_1>0, set f̂=0, check φ_1=0 → SC violated. Demonstrated in TH-IMP-501 proof sketch.

justifications.clarity: Σ_i φ_i = f̂(x) is one equation and trivially parseable.
justifications.novelty: Standard Shapley axiom from Shapley 1953; not new in 2026.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites Shapley 1953 and Lundberg-Lee 2017; experimentally validated in industrial SHAP implementations.
justifications.actionability: A unit test summing attribution outputs and comparing to f̂(x) is one line of code.

justifications.clarity: Clear in intent; the formal ∀ a, b clause is slightly verbose but unambiguous.
justifications.novelty: Classical Shapley 1953; restatement in attribution context.
justifications.actionability: Testable: swap features i, j across many inputs and check attribution equality.

justifications.clarity: If f̂ doesn't depend on X_i, then φ_i = 0 — easy to read.
justifications.novelty: Classical; goes by many names (Dummy, Null-player, Sensitivity-b).
justifications.actionability: Test: take an input, vary X_i while holding others, check φ_i is unchanged, then φ_i must equal 0.

justifications.clarity: Monotonicity statement is clear once you know v and v̂.
justifications.internal_consistency: Sound; well-formed implication with strict inequality.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites L17; status=seed suggests it has not been independently verified.
justifications.actionability: Testable: perturb v̂ so all marginal contributions rise; check φ_i non-decreasing.

justifications.clarity: No agent prefers another's bundle — one sentence, one equation.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Strong: Foley 1967, Thomson 2023, Rawlsian philosophical tradition.
justifications.actionability: Test: for each pair (i, j), compute u_i(alloc_i) and u_i(alloc_j), check ≥.

justifications.clarity: Clear: when population shrinks, remaining agents shouldn't be hurt.
justifications.novelty: Thomson 2023; standard in mechanism design but a useful reframing.
justifications.internal_consistency: Sound; the S ⊂ S' clause is precise.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites Thomson 2023 Ch.4 only; no broader literature listed.
justifications.actionability: Testable: vary the population, recompute allocations, check remaining-agent utilities.

justifications.clarity: Truth-telling is dominant strategy — universally familiar.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites GS 1973 and Thomson 2023 Ch.10; rational_choice philosophical tradition.
justifications.actionability: Test: try deviating from truthful report for each agent; utility should not increase.

justifications.clarity: Long NL but each conjunct is well-defined; proof sketch clarifies.
justifications.novelty: Novel impossibility that combines SC with classic Shapley axioms.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Proof is by construction; primary source is internal memo; Heskes 2020 cited as related.
justifications.actionability: The counter-example f(X)=βX_1, f̂=0 is directly reproducible.

justifications.clarity: Single-sentence corollary; clear consequence of TH-IMP-501.
justifications.novelty: Specific application to TreeSHAP; useful but not surprising given TH-IMP-501.
justifications.internal_consistency: Sound; logical consequence of the parent theorem.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Source is internal AXIOM_SKELETON §5.3; Heskes 2021 'implicit' — weak citation.
justifications.actionability: Testable: run TreeSHAP on the f̂=0 setup and observe φ_1=0.

justifications.clarity: Parallel structure to TH-COR-501-1; clear.
justifications.novelty: Same form, applied to KernelSHAP; not independent novelty.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Same internal source; weak external citation.

justifications.clarity: Clear about what Information Shapley uses (v(S)=E[Y|X_S]-E[Y]).
justifications.novelty: Application to Information Shapley; follows the same pattern.

justifications.clarity: Lists three methods that satisfy SC; readable.
justifications.novelty: Positive result; useful counterpoint to TH-IMP-501.
justifications.actionability: Testable by implementing Structural Shapley and checking.

justifications.clarity: States the precise case (f̂=f) clearly.
justifications.novelty: Useful proposition: SC alone is weak; novel observation about the relationship between SC and Dummy.
justifications.internal_consistency: Status='sketch' but logical structure is sound.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Status='sketch' is acknowledged weakness; only internal source.
justifications.actionability: Sketch-level proof; not directly runnable without expansion.

justifications.clarity: Claim is that SC and Dummy are independent when f̂≠f — clear but requires familiarity.
justifications.novelty: A genuine insight about orthogonality of axioms targeting different objects.
justifications.actionability: Indirect: one would need to construct cases where SC holds but Dummy fails, and vice versa.

justifications.clarity: Scenario B is well-defined (f̂≠f, SI>0, ∂f̂=0).
justifications.novelty: Identifies the 'entry point' to the impossibility; pedagogically useful.
justifications.actionability: Testable: instantiate Scenario B and verify the impossibility manifests.

justifications.clarity: Claim that SC + Dummy = structural completeness; phrase 'one-to-one correspondence' is vague.
justifications.actionability: Indirect; would require constructing a general framework.

justifications.clarity: Theorem statement is canonical and crystal-clear.
justifications.internal_consistency: process_meta flags circular reasoning ('additive already presupposes SHAP-like') — internal tension noted.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites L17 only; no alternative derivation.

justifications.clarity: Crystal clear: 'axioms should explain solutions to users'.
justifications.novelty: The user-facing framing of axioms is a real methodological contribution.
justifications.internal_consistency: Stance is internally consistent and well-argued.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites Procaccia, the Spliddit deployment (124k users), and the AAAI 2013 origin story.
justifications.actionability: Concrete applicability field: write a 1-2 sentence user explanation.

justifications.clarity: Argument is clearly structured (First/Secondly) and the verdict pattern is concrete.
justifications.novelty: Reframes Dummy as harmful in some contexts — a non-trivial methodological point.
justifications.internal_consistency: Sound argument from premise to conclusion.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Source: Ch.25 of Future of Economic Design; camel-allegory origin story; well-anchored.
justifications.actionability: Two-channel test is concrete and runnable.

justifications.clarity: Engineer > pure mathematician (within economic design) — clean stance.
justifications.novelty: Reframes the role of the economist; useful.
justifications.internal_consistency: Sound; one might disagree with the value priority but it is internally consistent.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites Roth's deferred acceptance as concrete example.
justifications.actionability: The hospital-administrator litmus test is operational.

justifications.clarity: No axiom is sacred — explicit rhetorical escalation via 'even'.
justifications.novelty: Strong methodological claim; well-known but worth restating.
justifications.actionability: Context-question is concrete: 'why this axiom in this planner's problem?'.

justifications.clarity: Nuanced grading > yes/no — clear methodological preference.
justifications.novelty: Parameterization as a design principle; not new but articulated well.
justifications.actionability: Operational: replace binary with degree 0-1 + intermediate threshold.

justifications.clarity: Existence > uniqueness — inversion of academic priority, sharply stated.
justifications.actionability: Reorder your result reporting: existence first, uniqueness second.

justifications.clarity: Help the weak and disabled — universally understood.
justifications.empirical_grounding: User-provided; high cross-cultural consistency claim but no specific citations.

justifications.clarity: Cannot kill — unambiguous.
justifications.novelty: Universal prohibition across cultures; not new.
justifications.actionability: Low; needs context (war, self-defense) to be testable.

justifications.clarity: Honesty / 不欺骗 — clear.
Fairness / 公平不偏私 — clear, though 'fairness' has many operationalizations.
Reproducibility / 可复现 — operationally clear in scientific contexts.
justifications.actionability: High — re-running the experiment is a direct test.

justifications.clarity: Teachability — clear in intent but undefined (teachable to whom?).
justifications.actionability: Medium — could measure student performance but requires setup.

justifications.clarity: Truth (empirical) / 对应现实 — clear as a stance.
Rawlsian justice / 罗尔斯正义论 — depends on familiarity with the tradition.
justifications.actionability: Low — needs a veil-of-ignorance setup to test.

justifications.clarity: Well-known paper; clear contributions listed.
justifications.internal_consistency: process_meta flags a possible circularity in 'uniqueness' — internal tension noted.

justifications.clarity: Heskes 2021 book Ch.21; structural importance well-defined.
justifications.actionability: Testable; structural importance can be computed on causal models.

justifications.clarity: Causal attribution framing is precise.
Comprehensive treatise; chapters organize axioms systematically.
justifications.novelty: Synthesizes a 50-year literature; high impact, low 'first-time' novelty.
justifications.actionability: Each chapter's axioms are testable in their respective domain.

justifications.clarity: Specialized tournament/axiomatic work; not as accessible as L17.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Less cited than L17 but established.

justifications.clarity: STY17 axiomatic framing is canonical.
justifications.internal_consistency: Sound in its own framework; tension with SC noted in process_meta of AX-SC-001.
justifications.actionability: Testable; many SHAP variants compared in the paper.

justifications.clarity: Three travelers, cost c(N); universally understood.
justifications.actionability: Testable: instantiate cost function and run Shapley.

justifications.clarity: Divorce asset allocation — clearly described.
Hospital cost-sharing — clear domain.
justifications.novelty: Real-world cost-sharing problem; not novel but relevant.

justifications.clarity: ML attribution with SHAP — well-defined.
justifications.actionability: Directly testable with any ML model.

justifications.clarity: Voting/election — universally familiar.
justifications.actionability: Testable with synthetic or real preference profiles.

justifications.clarity: FAA landing slot allocation — concrete domain.
Informational efficiency vs IC — clear tradeoff.
Efficiency vs equity — universally understood.
Simplicity vs faithfulness — clear but specific to ML/explanation.
justifications.actionability: Testable via approximation-error vs model-complexity curves.

justifications.clarity: Predictive accuracy vs structural faithfulness — clearly stated.
justifications.actionability: Testable on synthetic data where f and f̂ diverge.

justifications.clarity: Elegance — abstract aesthetic; depends on the field.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Description is generic ('Examples of elegance in various contexts').

justifications.clarity: Power distribution — political-science term; cross-cultural consistency 'low'.
Cultural conventions — vague; cross-cultural consistency 'low' makes this hard to pin down.
Existentialism — broad philosophical label rather than an axiom.
justifications.actionability: Very low — existentialism is not testable.

justifications.clarity: Scalability — operationally meaningful in CS contexts.
Social welfare / 社会总福利 — clear aggregate.
Falsifiability — Popperian; operationally meaningful.
justifications.actionability: High — propose an experiment that could refute the claim.

justifications.clarity: v(S) = E[f̂(X) | X_S] — one line, one definition.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites L17 and SY19; widely used.

justifications.clarity: Features must be distinguishable — clear precondition.
justifications.novelty: Mildly novel as a standalone assumption.

justifications.clarity: Confession that 'uniform' is itself a choice; clear.
justifications.actionability: Operational: report the assumed distribution with each numeric verdict.

justifications.clarity: Operators may break axioms — sharp methodological claim.
justifications.actionability: Pair each operator with a property-preservation analysis.

justifications.clarity: Hierarchical 'stepping out' framing is clear via enumeration.
justifications.actionability: Testable: check whether the planner is embedded in a higher-level entity.

justifications.clarity: Economist maps, user chooses — sharp role separation.
justifications.empirical_grounding: Cites the cumulative list of users; Thomson Ch.10.1.
justifications.actionability: Operational: every new axiom must be anchored to a specific planner.

justifications.clarity: Sharp self-aware observation about academic aesthetic.
Sharp critique of iff-theorems as packaging.
justifications.actionability: Operational: report weakest-list-for-uniqueness and strongest-list-for-satisfaction separately.

justifications.clarity: Interpretability — operationally meaningful in ML.
justifications.actionability: Medium — proxy measures exist (e.g., feature importance stability).

justifications.clarity: Efficiency — operationally meaningful.
Respect autonomy — clear moral concept.
Kantian ethics — requires philosophical background.
justifications.actionability: Low — categorical imperative tests are subtle.

justifications.clarity: Long-term interest — clear temporal scope.
justifications.actionability: Medium — discount-rate sensitivity can be measured.

justifications.clarity: Simplicity — operationally meaningful (Occam).
Removal-based explanations are precise.
Causal Shapley framing is clear.
Identifies problems with SHAP clearly.
Canonical; very clear.
justifications.internal_consistency: process_meta flags internal circularity; slightly downgraded.

justifications.clarity: Comparative axiomatic analysis.
circular: Defines 'good' in terms of itself; no information; trivially true and useless.

items.nl: An axiom is good if and only if it satisfies the criteria for being a good axiom.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Empty gesture; 'meaningful' and 'appropriate' are undefined; nothing to test.

items.nl: Allocations should be fair in a meaningful and appropriate way.

justifications.clarity: contradictory: Direct self-contradiction: A > B and B > A cannot both hold.

items.nl: For any mechanism, efficiency is more important than fairness, and fairness is more important than efficiency.

justifications.clarity: type_mismatch: Restates SP but quantifies over 'all games'; SP only makes sense in mechanism-de

items.nl: Strategy-proofness: Truth-telling is a dominant strategy in all games.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Pure slogan; no formal statement; no way to test; nothing novel.

items.nl: Fairness is important and should be respected.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Built on undefined predicates ~ and ≁; reads as a slogan but is operationally em

items.nl: Every agent should be treated the same as every other agent unless they are different in which case they should be treated differently.

justifications.clarity: circular: Honesty defined in terms of 'telling the truth', which is itself undefined; rest

items.nl: An attribution is honest if it tells the truth about what features matter.

justifications.clarity: restatement: This is literally Lundberg-Lee 2017's Local Accuracy / AX-SHAP-EFF, restated wit

items.nl: Axiom (SHAP, rediscovery): Σ_i φ_i = f̂(x).

justifications.clarity: premise_mismatch: Vaguely resembles AX-SC-001 (Structural Consistency) but lacks the crucial f vs

items.nl: If a feature is important then the attribution must reflect that importance.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Deserve is undefined; reads as ethics slogan, not testable axiom.

items.nl: No agent should ever receive less than they deserve.

justifications.clarity: contradictory: Internally inconsistent with AX-SHAP-DUM (Dummy); if f̂ doesn't depend on X_i th

items.nl: The attribution φ_i must always be greater than zero for every feature and every input.

justifications.clarity: vacuous: Plainly false; quantifier mismatch (X and y have different types); makes no clai

items.nl: For all X and all y, X = y.

justifications.clarity: vacuous: Asserts nothing of substance; no formal structure; no test.

items.nl: An axiom is true.

justifications.clarity: premise_mismatch: Conjunction of standard properties with no source; ok in isolation, but as a sin

items.nl: Allocations should be Pareto efficient, ex-post IC, and individually rational in expectation.

justifications.clarity: no_source: Declaration of independence excerpt; not an axiom; no formal content; not testab

items.nl: We hold these truths to be self-evident.

justifications.clarity: circular: Defines 'useful' in terms of being an axiom; circular.

items.nl: An axiom should be useful because it is an axiom.

justifications.clarity: vacuous: Universally false (Shapley axioms are not equivalent); no source; no test.

items.nl: All axioms are equivalent.

justifications.clarity: premise_mismatch: Vaguely gestures at SC but writes φ = f (which is dimensionally wrong; attributi

items.nl: Feature attributions should match the underlying data structure.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Defers to undefined 'reasonable person' standard; no source; not testable.

items.nl: An axiom is justified if a reasonable person would agree with it.

justifications.clarity: restatement: Garbled version of TH-SHAP-UNIQ; efficiency alone does not characterize Shapley

items.nl: If a Shapley value satisfies efficiency, then it is the unique Shapley value.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Defines 'fair' as 'accepted'; circular in mechanism design where you need to con

items.nl: The fair allocation is the one that all parties accept.

justifications.clarity: restatement: Slogan version of AX-THOMSON-STRPRO without citation; not novel; somewhat testab

items.nl: Mechanisms should be incentive compatible.

justifications.clarity: no_source: Meta-claim about axioms rather than an axiom; confuses popularity with quality;

items.nl: The best axiom is the one with the most citations.

justifications.clarity: no_source: Appeals to authority rather than argument; no source beyond the paper itself; co

items.nl: If an axiom is in the Lundberg-Lee paper then it is valid.

justifications.clarity: vacuous: Law of excluded middle; true but vacuous; not an axiom of any domain; restatemen

items.nl: An axiom must be either true or false but never both.

justifications.clarity: no_source: Unqualified 'complete' — complete in what sense? No reference; contradicts TH-IM

items.nl: Shapley axioms are complete.

justifications.clarity: vague_gesture: Axiom based on mood; anti-rational; not testable.

items.nl: We should add new axioms whenever we feel like it.

justifications.clarity: contradictory: Asserts biconditional between two unrelated concepts; not even well-formed for e

items.nl: Efficiency means nothing if not coupled with justice, and justice means nothing if not coupled with efficiency.

justifications.clarity: circular: Defines axiom as what axiom-makers make; circular definition; no information.

items.nl: Axioms are statements that axiom-makers make.

justifications.clarity: circular: Defines 'good' in terms of itself via reification; vacuous.

items.nl: An axiom is good if it has the property of goodness.
